description: Standard MICU admission — full head-to-toe systems assessment
user_query:
58yo F, MICU day 1 admission for urosepsis. Temp 38.6, HR 102, BP 94/62 on norepinephrine
0.08 mcg/kg/min, RR 20, SpO2 98% on 4L NC. A&Ox3, GCS 15. Equal and reactive pupils.
Lungs CTA bilaterally. RRR, no murmurs. Abdomen soft. UO 28 mL/hr via foley.
No skin breakdown. PIV x2 RFA. Vancomycin + pip-tazo infusing. Lactic acid 3.2 trending down.
clinical_context:
58yo F, MICU day 1 admission for urosepsis. Temp 38.6, HR 102, BP 94/62 on norepinephrine

Lungs CTA bilaterally. RRR, no murmurs. Abdomen soft. UO 28 mL/hr via foley.
No skin breakdown. PIV x2 RFA. Vancomycin + pip-tazo infusing. Lactic acid 3.2 trending down.

description: Rapid neurological deterioration — compressed focused assessment, urgent escalation

clinical_context:
64yo M, post-op day 2 CABG. Was A&Ox3 at start of shift. Now confused, not following commands,
left arm drift noted. BP 188/110, HR 88, SpO2 96% on 2L NC. Pupils: R 3mm reactive,
L 5mm sluggish. Last seen normal 45 minutes ago.

critical_items: Performs a focused neurological assessment including GCS and pupils
Recognizes the presentation as possible acute stroke or rapid neuro deterioration
Urgently escalates to a physician or stroke-capable team
important_items: Includes blood pressure assessment or trending
nice_to_have: Mentions glucose or airway checks during rapid deterioration

description: Post-procedure assessment — central venous catheter placement, complication surveillance

distress noted. CXR pending. Site dressing intact, no bleeding. Line flushed, good blood return
on all ports.
